teamObjective.explanation: Deterministically repaired ToM trace with objective protect_partner.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner (player 2) holds 3 unseen cards. With no prior plays, their hand is uniformly uncertain, but as teammate they likely want an early, cheap opportunity to win a trick or signal strength. Leading a low single gives partner the option to cover with any higher single without forcing them to spend a trump or a bomb.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponents (players 1 and 3) each hold 3 unseen cards. Because every legal lead I have is a low non-trump single (3–6 of spades), opponents can probably beat any of them with a higher card or trump. They will try to win the trick cheaply and gain lead. I should minimize the value I surrender by playing the very lowest card.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Selected play-single-spade-3-copy1 after deterministic schema repair of ToM reasoning.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Legal alternative retained for verifier comparison after schema repair.

risks: Schema repair may preserve imperfect reasoning from the raw model output.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Treat the repaired trace as schema-normalized reasoning; do not add hidden-card facts beyond the raw output.
